headline: Paramount Loses Second President in Two Years Amid Skydance Merger Turbulence
summary: Jeff Shell has stepped down as Paramount's president, marking another senior departure as the Skydance merger reshapes the company's leadership structure. Shell, a former NBCUniversal CEO, had joined Paramount only in 2024 as part of a broader turnaround effort. The continued executive churn signals strategic instability at a major media property navigating cord-cutting pressures and streaming consolidation.
oci_implication: Paramount's ongoing restructuring creates a window for cloud infrastructure conversations — media companies in transition frequently revisit vendor relationships and IT spend. OCI should ensure it has active engagement with Paramount's technical leadership as new decision-makers settle in.
